text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously mixed. No major macro shock today, but Qualcomm's AI/data center deal signals the chip-and-AI trade still has legs - that's the real driver for your kind of names right now.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem - down 12% and it's your largest position, so on margin that hole is real. PLTR is down nearly 10% and sitting well above its $85 stop, but worth watching if sentiment shifts. CRWD is also down over 10% - three names bleeding double digits at once on margin is a compounding drag. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data - a small move can spike fast, and margin makes that worse.

Watch: 1) AVGO specifically - largest position, biggest loss, trim trigger is already on. 2) Whether Qualcomm's AI infrastructure deal sparks any read-through momentum into AVGO, SMH, or CRWD.

Bottom line: Three double-digit losers on margin is your real risk - AVGO needs a decision today, not tomorrow.
